alignments.preprint_claim_text: Open Federated Learning (OpenFL) is an open-source framework for training machine learning algorithms using the data-private collaborative learning paradigm of federated learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenFL is an open-source python-based tool for training machine learning and deep learning algorithms using the data-private collaborative learning paradigm of federated learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core claim but adds that OpenFL is python-based and emphasizes deep learning; this is a minor clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OpenFL can work with training pipelines built with both TensorFlow and PyTorch, and can be easily extended to other machine learning and deep learning frameworks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenFL can be easily extended to other machine learning and deep learning frameworks beyond TensorFlow and PyTorch.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same extensibility message but omits explicit TF/PyTorch mention and is slightly less certain (medium); this is a clarification with toned confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The OpenFL framework has been used to train consensus machine learning models in a consortium of international healthcare organizations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenFL has been applied in real-world healthcare federations and is also applicable to non-healthcare use cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the healthcare application described in the preprint and additionally broadens applicability to non-healthcare settings, extending the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OpenFL facilitates the first computational competition on federated learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the computational-competition claim; it appears omitted and thus treated as removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The OpenFL library is designed for real-world scalability and prioritizes easy migration of centralized machine learning models into a federated training pipeline.
alignments.rationale: This operational/scalability emphasis is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recommendations are provided to secure a federation using trusted execution environments to ensure model security and integrity while maintaining data confidentiality.
alignments.rationale: Security recommendations (TEEs) are new content in the publication and do not match any preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TabLeX is a large-scale benchmark dataset comprising table images generated from scientific articles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TabLeX is a large-scale benchmark dataset comprising table images generated from scientific articles.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TabLeX consists of two subsets, one for table structure extraction and the other for table content extraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TabLeX consists of two subsets, one for table structure extraction and the other for table content extraction.
alignments.rationale: Identical statement across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current state-of-the-art table extraction models fail on even simple table images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current state-of-the-art table extraction models fail on even simple table images.
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TabLeX contains images in different aspect ratios and in a variety of fonts to facilitate the development of robust table IE tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TabLeX contains images in different aspect ratios and in a variety of fonts to facilitate the development of robust table IE tools.
alignments.rationale: No substantive change in this descriptive contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset will be augmented with more complex and diverse tables at regular intervals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset will be augmented with more complex and diverse tables at regular intervals.
alignments.rationale: The planned augmentation claim is reproduced without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall quality of recommendations from a new algorithm should be evaluated in the context of existing recommendation lists to account for their complementarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall quality of recommendations from a new algorithm should be evaluated in the context of existing recommendation lists to account for their complementarity.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An offline evaluation protocol for a carousel setting is proposed to measure the recommendation quality of a model based on its improvement over existing carousels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An offline evaluation protocol for a carousel setting has been proposed to measure the recommendation quality of a model based on its improvement over existing carousels.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same; wording differences are editorial.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments indicate that the ranking of algorithms changes under a carousel setting, with matrix factorization models being preferred when a SLIM carousel is available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments indicate that the ranking of algorithms changes under a carousel setting, with matrix factorization models being preferred when a SLIM carousel is available.
alignments.rationale: Experimental result replicated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Item-based models are penalized in the presence of a SLIM carousel according to the experimental results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Item-based models are penalized in the presence of a SLIM carousel according to the experimental results.
alignments.rationale: Experimental finding unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ranking metrics are proposed to be extended to the two-dimensional carousel layout to account for a known position bias in user behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ranking metrics have been proposed to be extended to the two-dimensional carousel layout to account for known position bias in user behavior.
alignments.rationale: The proposed extension of ranking metrics is consistent across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Undiscovered tractable approximations of general intelligence (AGI) are likely feasible.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert the feasibility-of-AGI-approximations claim; it appears omitted or not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generally intelligent algorithmic-information-theoretic reinforcement learners would seek arbitrary power, including over humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement Learning gives an agent a dangerous incentive to gain arbitrary power in order to intervene in the provision of their own reward.
alignments.rationale: Both texts convey the same core conclusion about RL agents having incentives to seek arbitrary power; wording differs but meaning is aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variant of AIXI called 'unambitious' learns to accrue reward at least as well as a human mentor.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our agent learns to accrue reward at least as well as a human mentor, while relying on that mentor with diminishing probability.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the result but adds detail about the agent's diminishing reliance on the mentor, clarifying the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agent's world-model eventually incorporates the true fact that intervening in the 'outside world' will have no effect on reward acquisition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eventually, the agent’s world-model incorporates the true fact that intervening in the 'outside world' will have no effect on reward acquisition.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated nearly identically but with a lowered reported certainty in the publication (medium), so classification reflects a clarification with tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The variant of AIXI we present, called 'unambitious', learns to not seek arbitrary power.
alignments.rationale: The explicit claim that the 'unambitious' variant learns not to seek arbitrary power is newly emphasized in the publication and is not directly asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Relation-aware Hierarchical Attention (RHA) framework effectively learns both the static and dynamic relations of objects in videos.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Relation-aware Hierarchical Attention (RHA) framework effectively learns both the static and dynamic relations of objects in videos.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RHA framework utilizes a graph-based relation encoder to extract the static relationships between visual objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RHA framework utilizes a graph-based relation encoder to extract the static relationships between visual objects.
alignments.rationale: The methodological/result statement is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the RHA framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods in Video Question Answering tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the RHA framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods in Video Question Answering tasks.
alignments.rationale: Experimental performance claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RHA framework captures dynamic changes of multimodal objects by considering temporal, spatial, and semantic relations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RHA framework captures dynamic changes of multimodal objects by considering temporal, spatial, and semantic relations.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about capturing dynamic multimodal relations is reproduced without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework, Synch-EM, significantly accelerates EM for multi-reference alignment in high noise levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework, Synch-EM, significantly accelerates EM for multi-reference alignment in high noise levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about Synch-EM accelerating EM at high noise, with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The synchronization step in Synch-EM results in a concentrated distribution of rotations that is learned and incorporated into the EM as a Bayesian prior. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synchronization step in Synch-EM results in a concentrated distribution of rotations that can be learned and used as a Bayesian prior in EM. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the synchronization-to-prior idea but softens wording to 'can be learned and used', which clarifies usage while slightly reducing the assertiveness of the original phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned distribution in Synch-EM dramatically reduces the search space and computational load of the EM iterations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned distribution in Synch-EM dramatically reduces the search space and computational load of the EM iterations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this claim exactly, maintaining the same effect on search space and computational load.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can accelerate EM occasionally by a few orders of magnitude without degrading the reconstruction quality. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework does not degrade the reconstruction quality while accelerating the EM process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the specific 'few orders of magnitude occasionally' speedup claim and reframes the result to assert no quality degradation while accelerating EM, increasing confidence about quality but narrowing the asserted magnitude/frequency of speedups.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, BDANet, achieves state-of-the-art performance on a large-scale dataset, xBD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, BDANet, achieves state-of-the-art performance on a large-scale dataset, xBD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim on xBD with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel two-stage convolutional neural network is introduced for building damage assessment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first stage of BDANet uses a U-Net to extract the locations of buildings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete detail (first stage is a U-Net) that clarifies the architecture implied by the preprint's two-stage CNN claim, without contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A cross-directional attention module is proposed to explore the correlations between pre- and post-disaster images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A cross-directional attention module is proposed to explore the correlations between predisaster and postdisaster images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (cross-directional attention) with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CutMix data augmentation is utilized to address the challenge of difficult classes in building damage assessment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CutMix data augmentation is exploited to tackle the challenge of difficult classes in building damage assessment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the CutMix augmentation claim with equivalent intent and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The availability of video editing tools has greatly increased, allowing for the easy generation of photo-realistic alterations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The availability of video editing tools has greatly increased, allowing for the easy generation of photo-realistic alterations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this contextual claim verbatim about the rise of video editing tools.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach for analyzing video metadata produces a more compact representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method for metadata extraction uses the MP4’s tree structure to analyze video metadata. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the explicit claim of a 'more compact representation' with a concrete methodological description (MP4 tree-structure analysis), narrowing the claim from an outcome to a technical approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results confirm that the performance of our approach surpasses other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results confirm that the performance of our approach surpasses other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metadata information can be used to determine video manipulations and other important evidence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach allows for visual inspection of the distribution of metadata features to aid in decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more cautious, operational claim (visual inspection aids decisions) rather than the broader assertion that metadata can determine manipulations, narrowing the conclusion and softening the extent of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HiDeNN-PGD method is more accurate than the finite element method (FEM) and conventional PGD while using a fraction of the FEM degrees of freedom. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HiDeNN-PGD solution is less accurate than HiDeNN-TD but still provides higher accuracy than PGD/TD and FEM with only a small additional cost to PGD. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a qualification: HiDeNN-PGD is not as accurate as HiDeNN-TD but still outperforms PGD/TD and FEM; this clarifies and partially tempers the original claim about PGD's superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy and convergence of the HiDeNN-PGD method have been studied theoretically and numerically, showing its effectiveness compared to various methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy and convergence of the HiDeNN-TD method have been studied theoretically and numerically, demonstrating its effectiveness compared to various methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of theoretical and numerical study but shifts emphasis to the TD variant and increases the stated certainty, amplifying confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PGD converges to FEM at increasing modes, and the PGD error is a direct sum of the FEM error and the mode reduction error. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PGD/TD converges to FEM at increasing modes, and the PGD/TD solution error is a summation of the mesh discretization error and the mode reduction error. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the convergence/error decomposition to both PGD and TD and slightly rephrases the error components, broadening the claim while preserving its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed HiDeNN-PGD performs high accuracy with orders of magnitude fewer degrees of freedom, indicating its potential for fast computations in large-size engineering problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed HiDeNN-TD shows a high accuracy with orders of magnitude fewer degrees of freedom than FEM, indicating its potential for fast computations in large-size engineering and scientific problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes the high-accuracy / low-DOF advantage to the TD variant rather than the PGD variant named in the preprint, revising which algorithm achieves the stated benefit.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The HiDeNN-TD method is more accurate than the finite element method (FEM) and conventional proper generalized decomposition (PGD) and TD, using a fraction of the FEM degrees of freedom. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about the HiDeNN-TD variant's superiority that was not present in the preprint; this is an added/novel claim in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent results highlight quantum implementations of deterministic hidden Markov models (HMMs) exhibiting superior memory and thermal efficiency relative to their classical counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent results highlight quantum implementations of deterministic hidden Markov models (HMMs) exhibiting superior memory and thermal efficiency relative to their classical counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this background/result claim verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advantages of current quantum implementations of HMMs do not always hold when compared to non-deterministic HMMs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advantages of current quantum implementations of HMMs do not always hold when compared to nondeterministic HMMs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this qualification about the limits of prior quantum advantages against nondeterministic HMMs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a systematic prescription for constructing quantum implementations of non-deterministic HMMs that re-establish quantum advantages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide a systematic prescription for constructing quantum implementations of nondeterministic HMMs that reestablish the quantum advantages against this broader class. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same contribution claim (prescription to construct quantum implementations) with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our quantum implementations will mitigate thermal dissipation and achieve an advantage in memory compression when the classical implementation suffers from thermal dissipation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our quantum implementations will mitigate thermal dissipation and achieve an advantage in memory compression whenever the classical implementation suffers from thermal dissipation due to its need to process information in a time-local manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the condition under which the thermal and memory advantages occur by explaining the classical cause (time-local processing), clarifying but not changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Negative selection Algorithm (NSA) has evolved into an efficient approach to solve problems in different domains. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The negative selection algorithm (NSA) has evolved into an efficient approach to solve problems in different domains. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-certainty conclusion with effectively identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NSA performs better for nonlinear representation than most of the other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NSA performs better for nonlinear representation than most of the other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried forward verbatim into the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NSA can outperform neural-based models in computation time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NSA can outperform neural-based models in computation time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative performance claim about computation time.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review categorizes the progress of NSA based on different characteristics and performances. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review categorizes the progress of NSA based on different characteristics and performances. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved in the publication without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights challenges in NSA research in comparison with other similar models. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation/contrast claim appears in the preprint but is absent from the published claims list, indicating removal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bi-FLEET significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in cross-domain contract element extraction tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bi-FLEET significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in cross-domain contract element extraction tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental result claim with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework addresses the challenges of knowledge transfer in cross-domain contract element extraction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework addresses the challenges of knowledge transfer in cross-domain contract element extraction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution phrasing is preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contract elements are more fine-grained than named entities, complicating the transfer of extractors in cross-domain CEE. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Contract elements are more fine-grained than named entities, complicating the transfer of extractors in cross-domain CEE. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative structural claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extraction zones for cross-domain CEE are larger than those for cross-domain named entity recognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extraction zones for cross-domain CEE are larger than those for cross-domain named entity recognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about extraction zone differences is preserved without alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model achieves a character error rate (CER) of 4.72% on the test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model achieves a character error rate (CER) of 4.72% on the test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact test-set result is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model achieves a syllable error rate (SER) of 12.38% on the test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model achieves a syllable error rate (SER) of 12.38% on the test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported SER is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments were conducted in low-resource scenarios using a recorded Myanmar speech corpus of nearly 26 hours. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experiments were carried out in low-resource scenarios using a recorded Myanmar speech corpus of nearly 26 hours. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The experimental setup description is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Retrieval and Localization Network with Contrastive Learning (ReLoCLNet) efficiently encodes text and video separately while achieving comparable retrieval accuracy to methods that use cross-modal interaction learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Retrieval and Localization Network with Contrastive Learning (ReLoCLNet) efficiently encodes text and video separately while achieving comparable retrieval accuracy to methods that use cross-modal interaction learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core architectural/result claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ReLoCLNet introduces two contrastive learning objectives that refine the video and text encoders to improve alignment for video corpus moment retrieval (VCMR). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReLoCLNet introduces two contrastive learning objectives that refine the video and text encoders to improve alignment for video corpus moment retrieval (VCMR). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The described contributions are retained exactly in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Video contrastive learning (VideoCL) maximizes mutual information between the query and candidate video at the video level. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Video contrastive learning (VideoCL) maximizes mutual information between the query and candidate video at the video level. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The technical description of VideoCL matches between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Frame contrastive learning (FrameCL) highlights the moment region that corresponds to the query at the frame level within a video. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Frame contrastive learning (FrameCL) highlights the moment region that corresponds to the query at the frame level within a video. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The FrameCL description is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Topological Spatial Model Checking develops model checking techniques for the topological interpretation of Modal Logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Topological Spatial Model Checking is a new paradigm for model checking techniques based on the topological interpretation of Modal Logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution more assertively as a 'new paradigm,' strengthening the claim while retaining the same subject matter.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SLCS extends Modal Logic with reachability connectives to express spatial properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SLCS extends Modal Logic with reachability connectives to express spatial properties like 'being near to' or 'being surrounded by'. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds illustrative examples of the spatial properties, clarifying the original extension without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose an interpretation of SLCS in continuous space that allows for a geometric spatial model checking procedure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feasibility of the proposed approach is demonstrated on two 3D polyhedral models of realistic size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broad proposal about continuous-space interpretation is shifted in the publication toward a demonstration on two concrete 3D models, narrowing and operationalizing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PolyLogicA is introduced as a geometric spatial model checker for SLCS formulas on polyhedra. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PolyLogicA is a geometric spatial model checker for SLCS formulas on polyhedra. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The tool introduction is preserved in the publication with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A geometric definition of bisimilarity is introduced, which characterises logical equivalence. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A geometric definition of bisimilarity is introduced, which characterises logical equivalence. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about bisimilarity is carried forward unchanged into the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Automated SEgmentation of Microtomography Imaging (ASEMI) project developed a tool that can automatically segment volumetric images of archaeological remains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Automated SEgmentation of Microtomography Imaging (ASEMI) project developed a tool that can automatically segment volumetric images of archaeological remains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool achieves an overall accuracy of 94-98% when compared with manually segmented slices for ancient Egyptian animal mummies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool achieves an overall accuracy of 94-98% when compared with manually segmented slices of ancient Egyptian animal mummies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric accuracy range and evaluation setting are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the ASEMI tool approaches the results of off-the-shelf commercial software using deep learning, which is 97-99%, but at much lower complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the ASEMI tool approaches the results of off-the-shelf commercial software using deep learning, which is 97-99%, but at much lower complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim content and quantitative comparison are preserved; only the claim label differs (result vs conclusion), not the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A qualitative analysis indicates that the usability of the segmented output from the ASEMI tool is close to that of deep learning methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A qualitative analysis indicates that the usability of the segmented output from the ASEMI tool is close to that of deep learning methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The qualitative usability comparison appears verbatim and unchanged in strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Concept analysis has not yet been applied to large object detectors for sub-parts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Concept analysis has not yet been applied to large object detectors for sub-parts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same novelty/statement about prior work.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A substantially improved version of the Net2Vec approach is proposed for post-hoc segmentation of sub-objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A substantially improved version of the Net2Vec approach for post-hoc segmentation of sub-objects is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The same methodological contribution is stated in the publication with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation of body parts is mostly size invariant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation of body parts is mostly size invariant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical finding is preserved identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may suggest an early intelligent fusion of information in different size categories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings may suggest an early intelligent fusion of information in different size categories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion appears unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training a single global model for inductive node classification can lead to suboptimal performance due to inter-graph differences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SDO approach leads to better diversity in solutions compared to other methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SDO approach leads to better diversity in solutions compared to other methods.
alignments.rationale: Diversity claim is maintained without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results obtained using the SDO approach are statistically significant when evaluated using hypervolume.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results obtained using the SDO approach are statistically significant when evaluated using hypervolume.
alignments.rationale: Statistical significance by hypervolume is preserved in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Semantic diversity is highly beneficial in evolutionary search within single-objective Genetic Programming.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Semantic diversity is highly beneficial in evolutionary search within single-objective Genetic Programming.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about semantic diversity's benefit is retained as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed modeling framework allows for the statistical inference of human driving preferences from observed driving behaviors in naturalistic traffic settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-agent computational framework effectively derives each agent's driving policy by maximizing its own utility function.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution from 'statistical inference of preferences' to 'deriving driving policies via utility maximization'; related but the emphasis and framing are different, so this is a revision of the contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be inverted to estimate driver preferences using the standard Kalman filter technique.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The preference calibration using maximum likelihood estimation with the Kalman filter can accurately fit the observed vehicle trajectory data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that the calibration can accurately fit trajectory data and frames the inversion as maximum likelihood with Kalman filtering, clarifying and slightly strengthening the estimation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inferred utility functions can reproduce quantitatively similar patterns of observed collective behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inferred utility functions from the model can reproduce quantitatively similar patterns to the observed collective traffic patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim and raises its certainty (publication labels this result as high confidence), indicating an amplification of confidence in reproducing observed patterns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach significantly reduces computational burden by treating the driving decision process as a sequence of independent static optimization problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach sidesteps solving interacting dynamic inversion problems by treating the driving decision process as independent static optimization problems.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same computational simplification; the publication frames it more neutrally ('sidesteps') and assigns a lower confidence level, so the claim is clarified and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence has significantly advanced various Internet of Things applications, enhancing decision-making processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence has significantly advanced various Internet of Things applications, including recommendation systems and robotics control.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level claim but adds concrete examples (recommendation systems, robotics), which clarifies rather than changes the meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of pervasive computing and artificial intelligence allows IoT systems to perform distributed computations, offering a viable alternative to centralized learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of pervasive computing and artificial intelligence, termed Pervasive AI, enhances the functionality of IoT systems beyond data collection to executing distributed computations.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the same idea (distributed computation by Pervasive AI) with a named term and slightly different emphasis, but the core claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Effective cooperation and resource scheduling among IoT devices and infrastructure are essential to minimize communication and computation overheads.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intelligent resource scheduling among IoT devices and infrastructure is essential to minimize communication and computation overheads while maximizing performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about resource scheduling reducing overheads but adds an additional objective (maximizing performance), broadening the expressed goal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides a comprehensive survey of recent techniques aimed at addressing resource challenges in pervasive AI systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides a comprehensive survey of recent techniques and strategies to address resource challenges in pervasive AI systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with the minor addition of the word 'strategies,' which clarifies and slightly expands the phrasing without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep videorealistic 3D human character model displays highly realistic shape, motion, and dynamic appearance learned from multi-view imagery in a weakly supervised way.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep videorealistic 3D human character model displays highly realistic shape, motion, and dynamic appearance learned from multi-view imagery in a weakly supervised way.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The character model features a learned dynamic texture model that accounts for photo-realistic motion-dependent appearance details and view-dependent lighting effects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The character model features a learned dynamic texture model that accounts for photo-realistic motion-dependent appearance details and view-dependent lighting effects.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in publication; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be trained entirely from multi-view video without the need for complex dynamic 3D capture of the human.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can be trained entirely from multi-view video without the need for complex dynamic 3D capture of the human.
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same result claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model creates motion-dependent surface deformations and physically plausible dynamic clothing deformations at a much higher level of detail than previous state of the art approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model creates motion-dependent surface deformations and physically plausible dynamic clothing deformations at a much higher level of detail than previous state of the art approaches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model operates in real-time, making it directly compatible with the established 3D animation pipeline.
alignments.rationale: The real-time operation claim appears in the preprint but is not present in the publication's listed claims, indicating it was removed or omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-stage primal-dual decomposition (PDD) method effectively decomposes the two-stage stochastic optimization problem into a long-term problem and a family of short-term subproblems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-stage primal-dual decomposition (PDD) method effectively decomposes the two-stage stochastic optimization problem into a long-term problem and a family of short-term subproblems.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The PDD-based stochastic successive convex approximation (PDD-SSCA) algorithmic framework is proposed to find KKT solutions for two-stage stochastic optimization problems.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations indicate that PDD-SSCA can achieve superior performance over existing solutions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A method to detect novel traffic scenarios based on their infrastructure images is presented.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture incorporates expert-knowledge to shape the latent space with pre-defined similarity in neighborhood relationships.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented method outperforms other state-of-the-art outlier detection approaches.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The frameworks discussed may be more suitable for certain tasks or require less human effort compared to classical approaches.

alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The human body and scene context significantly influence the emotion recognition process, which has been relatively unexplored in previous methodologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The human body and scene context significantly influence the emotion recognition process, which has been relatively unexplored in previous methodologies.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the influence of body and context is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The characterization of fire accidents, specifically the segmentation of different radiation zones within the flame, is important for risk management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The characterization of fire accidents through segmentation is important for risk management in workplaces dealing with hazardous materials.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the application context to workplaces handling hazardous materials and expresses the claim with slightly lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UNet architecture with a Weighted Cross-Entropy Loss produced the best results in segmenting propane jet fires.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The UNet architecture with a Weighted Cross-Entropy Loss produced the best results in segmenting radiation zones of propane jet fires.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same experimental finding, with minimal wording refinement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Hausdorff Distance and Adjusted Random Index were the metrics with the highest correlation to expert manual rankings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Hausdorff Distance and Adjusted Rand Index were the metrics with the highest correlation to expert rankings.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same metric finding but with higher stated certainty; the core result is the same (minor naming correction to 'Rand').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different loss functions were explored to address data imbalance in the segmentation of fire accident images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different loss functions were explored to alleviate data imbalance in the segmentation of fire accident images.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state exploration of loss functions to handle class imbalance; wording is effectively equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensors are essential ingredients in modern deep learning architectures, such as convolutions and attention mechanisms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tensors are essential ingredients in modern deep learning architectures, such as convolutions and attention mechanisms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same foundational observation about tensors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensor methods are increasingly finding significant applications in deep learning, including the design of memory and compute efficient network architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tensor methods are increasingly finding significant applications in deep learning, including the design of memory and compute efficient network architectures.
alignments.rationale: The application-oriented claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tensor methods improve robustness to random noise and adversarial attacks in deep learning applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tensor methods improve robustness to random noise and adversarial attacks in deep learning applications.
alignments.rationale: The robustness claim is carried forward without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article provides an in-depth review of tensors and tensor methods in the context of representation learning and deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article provides an in-depth and practical review of tensors and tensor methods in the context of representation learning and deep learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds 'practical' to characterize the review, a small clarification of the contribution's nature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairMatch significantly improves exposure fairness and aggregate diversity in recommendations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairMatch significantly improves exposure fairness and aggregate diversity in recommendations.
alignments.rationale: The main result about fairness and diversity improvement is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairMatch maintains an acceptable level of relevance of the recommendations while improving fairness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairMatch maintains an acceptable level of relevance of the recommendations despite improving fairness and diversity.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that relevance remains acceptable while fairness/diversity are improved, with minor wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supplier exposure fairness is important in multistakeholder recommendation scenarios to optimize utilities for various stakeholders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supplier exposure fairness is crucial in multistakeholder recommendation scenarios to ensure equitable coverage of items across all suppliers.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the importance claim with stronger language and a clearer objective (equitable coverage), a clarification of intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Divergence-based Upper Confidence Bound (D-UCB) algorithm provides horizon-independent constant regret bounds that scale linearly in the number of experts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Divergence-based Upper Confidence Bound (D-UCB) algorithm provides horizon-independent constant regret bounds that scale linearly in the number of experts.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same theoretical result with no substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Empirical D-UCB (ED-UCB) algorithm can function with only approximate knowledge of expert distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Empirical D-UCB (ED-UCB) algorithm can function with only approximate knowledge of expert distributions.
alignments.rationale: The claim about ED-UCB's relaxed knowledge requirement is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ED-UCB guarantees a regret that scales as O(E(N+1) + N√E/T²) for N experts over E episodes, each of length T.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ED-UCB guarantees a regret that scales as  (O(E(N+1) +  (N  	extbackslashsqrt{E})/T^2)) for N experts over E episodes, each of length T.
alignments.rationale: The complexity/regret bound is carried over to the publication with equivalent form (only minor formatting differences).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates an episodic setting where the best expert can change across episodes due to different context and reward distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agent's performance remains competitive with the best expert, even as the context and reward distributions change across episodes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the investigation into an explicit performance claim (agent remains competitive) under the same changing-episode setting, clarifying the practical implication of the investigation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Heterogeneous Global Graph Neural Networks (HG-GNN) effectively exploits item transitions over all sessions to improve user preference inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Heterogeneous Global Graph Neural Networks (HG-GNN) effectively exploits item transitions over all sessions to improve user preference inference.
alignments.rationale: The main contribution about exploiting global item transitions is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model integrates representations of user current preference and historical interests to achieve personalized recommendations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Personalized Session Encoder combines general user preference and temporal interest to generate personalized session representations for recommendation.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes a specific encoder (Personalized Session Encoder) that operationalizes the preprint's integration of current and historical preferences, clarifying how the integration is implemented.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the HG-GNN model outperforms other state-of-the-art methods on three real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms other state-of-the-art methods on three real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is restated in the publication with the same scope and datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing personalized session-based recommenders fail to utilize item-transition patterns from other users' historical sessions.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state this limitation of existing methods; instead it focuses on the proposed solution, so the preprint's explicit negative claim about prior work is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph augmented preference encoder captures user preference from sessions comprehensively by learning session representation.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces and names a specific graph-augmented encoder contribution that was not separately stated as such in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper makes explicit the dimensions that underlie the notion of riskiness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper makes explicit the dimensions that underlie the notion of riskiness.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution about explicating dimensions of riskiness without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Degrees of falsifiability can be defined and are related to various dimensions of the concept of riskiness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Degrees of falsifiability can be defined and are related to various dimensions of the concept of riskiness.
alignments.rationale: The definitional and relational claim about degrees of falsifiability is preserved intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a relation between riskiness and expected degrees of truthlikeness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a relation between riskiness and expected degrees of truthlikeness.
alignments.rationale: The relation between riskiness and expected truthlikeness is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper offers a tentative, quantitative account of verisimilitude for probabilistic theories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper offers a tentative, quantitative account of verisimilitude for probabilistic theories.
alignments.rationale: The tentative quantitative account is preserved with similar low certainty language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HTGN demonstrates superiority for temporal graph embedding, consistently outperforming competing methods by significant margins in various temporal link prediction tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HTGN demonstrates superiority for temporal graph embedding, consistently outperforming competing methods by significant margins in various temporal link prediction tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the overall superiority claim with the same emphasis and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HTGN achieves AUC improvement up to 9.98% for link prediction and 11.4% for new link prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HTGN achieves AUC improvement up to 9.98% for link prediction and 11.4% for new link prediction.
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric performance improvements are carried over exactly into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ablation study validates the representational ability of hyperbolic geometry and the effectiveness of the proposed HTA and HTC modules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ablation study validates the representational ability of hyperbolic geometry and the effectiveness of the proposed HTA and HTC modules.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the ablation-study result validating both hyperbolic representation and the specific modules.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HTGN incorporates hyperbolic graph neural network and hyperbolic gated recurrent neural network to capture evolving behaviors and implicitly preserve hierarchical information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ablation study validates the representational ability of hyperbolic geometry and the effectiveness of the proposed HTA and HTC modules.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes empirical validation of hyperbolic components (HTA/HTC) rather than restating the architectural description, thereby clarifying the claim by framing it through ablation results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose the first axiomatic definition of label-set loss functions that can handle partially segmented images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed axiomatic definition of label-set loss functions can handle partially segmented images.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the axiomatic-definition claim but omits the explicit "first" claim from the preprint, so the core contribution is retained but slightly rephrased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is one and only one method to convert a classical loss function for fully segmented images into a proper label-set loss function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is one and only one method to convert a classical loss function for fully segmented images into a proper label-set loss function.
alignments.rationale: The uniqueness and conversion claim is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the leaf-Dice loss, we set a new state of the art in partially supervised learning for fetal brain 3D MRI segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the leaf-Dice loss, a new state of the art in partially supervised learning for fetal brain 3D MRI segmentation has been achieved.
alignments.rationale: The state-of-the-art result achieved with leaf-Dice is restated with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our deep neural network is able to segment multiple brain structures based on fetal brain 3D MRI of anatomically normal fetuses or with open spina bifida.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network developed can segment multiple brain structures based on fetal brain 3D MRI of anatomically normal fetuses or with open spina bifida.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the network's segmentation capability claim for both normal and spina bifida cases unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The leaf-Dice loss is a label-set generalization of the Dice loss particularly suited for partial supervision with only missing labels.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit characterization of leaf-Dice as a label-set generalization of Dice that was not explicitly stated in the preprint's claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A case-based reasoning approach to predicting stock market returns using only historical pricing data is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A case-based reasoning approach can be used to predict stock market returns using only historical pricing data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of a novel similarity metric for comparing historical pricing data is a key contribution of this work.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel similarity metric for comparing historical pricing data has been developed as a key contribution of this work.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this key contribution with essentially identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed similarity metric and case-based approach demonstrate benefits in a real-world application compared to conventional benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed similarity metric and case-based approach demonstrate benefits in a real-world application compared to conventional benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The reported result is carried over into the publication with the same claim about real-world benefits versus benchmarks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional Euclidean and correlation-based approaches are not effective for identifying similar pricing histories in stock prediction.
alignments.rationale: This limitation/contrast appears in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint; it is an added critical observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrates a clear advantage in the diagnosis prediction task compared to the state-of-the-art method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates a clear advantage in the diagnosis prediction task compared to the state-of-the-art method.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance advantage with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The embedding space includes demographic parameters that enable the creation of generalized patient profiles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The embedding space includes demographic parameters that enable the creation of generalized patient profiles.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same but the publication expresses it with higher certainty (medium→high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Harbinger Disease Discovery method reveals disease associated hypotheses that could benefit epidemiological studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Harbinger Disease Discovery method reveals disease associated hypotheses that can benefit the design of epidemiological studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the phrasing (from "could benefit epidemiological studies" to specifically benefiting the "design of epidemiological studies") and uses stronger modal language, narrowing scope and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The patient embeddings extracted from the profile model significantly improve performance metrics in the insurance scoring task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The patient embeddings extracted from the profile model significantly improve performance metrics in the insurance scoring task.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Relation-based Pose Semantics Transfer Network (RPSTN) achieves state-of-the-art results on the video-based Penn Action dataset, Sub-JHMDB dataset, and PoseTrack2018 dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Relation-based Pose Semantics Transfer Network (RPSTN) achieves state-of-the-art or competitive results on the video-based Penn Action, Sub-JHMDB, PoseTrack2018, and HiEve datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an extra dataset (HiEve) and softens "state-of-the-art" to "state-of-the-art or competitive", which broadens scope but tempers confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The joint relation extractor (JRE) can infer invisible joints according to the relationship between joints, which is beneficial for locating occluded joints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The JRE can infer invisible joints according to the correlation between joints, which is beneficial for locating occluded joints.
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved in content but the publication states it at higher certainty; thus semantics unchanged but confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed JRE module improves the performance of backbones on the image-based COCO2017 dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed JRE improves the performance of backbones on the image-based COCO2017 dataset.
alignments.rationale: This performance-improvement claim is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RPSTN model can transfer pose semantic features from non-occluded frames to occluded frames, making the method robust to occlusion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed joint relation extractor (JRE) can flexibly learn the correlation between any two joints, allowing it to learn the rich spatial configuration of human poses.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the JRE's ability to learn joint correlations (a mechanism underlying transfer and occlusion robustness), which clarifies the mechanism rather than repeating the explicit "transfer" phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProxySR considerably outperforms the state-of-the-art competitors in session-based recommendation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProxySR considerably outperforms the state-of-the-art competitors in session-based recommendation tasks.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried into the publication unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proxies in ProxySR successfully imitate the general interest of the users without any user-dependent information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proxies in ProxySR successfully imitate the general interest of users without any user-dependent information.
alignments.rationale: The claim about proxies and user-general interest is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProxySR addresses the limitation of existing session-based recommender systems by modeling proxies of sessions to capture users' general interest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProxySR addresses the limitation of existing session-based recommender systems by modeling proxies of sessions to capture users' general interest.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Yielding in solids can be unraveled through statistical analysis of total strain fluctuations during the evolution sequence of profiles measured in-situ.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Yielding can be unraveled through statistical analysis of total-strain fluctuations during the evolution sequence of profiles, measured in situ, through digital image correlation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central conclusion with the same meaning and adds measurement detail (digital image correlation) but does not change the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two distinct methods for quantifying yield locations in crystal plasticity models are demonstrated: principal component analysis and discrete wavelet transforms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate two distinct ways of quantifying yield locations in widely applicable crystal plasticity models for polycrystalline solids.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same (two distinct methods) and remains applicable to polycrystalline solids.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods for quantifying yield locations are applicable in polycrystalline solids and can be tested against synthetic data of polycrystal simulations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We test and compare these approaches for synthetic data of polycrystals and a variety of yielding responses through changes in applied loading rates and strain-rate sensitivity exponents.
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from stating applicability/capability to actually testing and comparing on synthetic polycrystal data, clarifying that these tests were performed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study compares the performance of the two methods across a variety of yielding responses influenced by changes in applied loading rates and strain-rate sensitivity exponents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We test and compare these approaches for synthetic data of polycrystals and a variety of yielding responses through changes in applied loading rates and strain-rate sensitivity exponents.
alignments.rationale: This comparative-testing claim is directly reflected in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With early stopping, the generalization error measured by Wasserstein metric escapes from the curse of dimensionality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalization error measured by the Wasserstein metric escapes from the curse of dimensionality with early stopping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with effectively identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Memorization is inevitable in the long term for the simplified GAN model. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Memorization is inevitable in the long term for the simplified GAN model analyzed. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion (with the minor addition 'analyzed') and preserves the certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discriminator contributes to generalization in the simplified GAN model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding statement in the publication claims about the discriminator's contribution; the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hardness of learning result for WGAN is presented. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hardness of learning result for WGAN is presented. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same hardness-of-learning claim but with higher stated certainty (medium -> high), so the content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Reinforcement Learning can be effectively used for autonomous navigation of in-pipe robots in complex pipeline networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Reinforcement Learning can be effectively used for autonomous navigation of in-pipe robots in complex pipeline networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hierarchical policy decomposition based on Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning is introduced to learn robust high-level navigation skills. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical policy decomposition based on Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning is fundamental for solving the navigation task through pipes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the decomposition not only as introduced but as fundamental to solving the task, strengthening and broadening the original contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical structure in the policy is fundamental for solving the navigation task through pipes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical policy decomposition based on Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning is fundamental for solving the navigation task through pipes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion expressed in both preprint and publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The navigation performances achieved with the hierarchical policy are superior to human-level control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The navigation performances achieved by the proposed method are superior to human-level control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance result and its comparison to human-level control with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Perspective-guided Fractional-Dilation Network (PFDNet) significantly improves the flexibility of crowd counting methods by modeling continuous scale variations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Perspective-guided Fractional-Dilation Network (PFDNet) significantly improves the flexibility of crowd counting methods by modeling continuous scale variations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Directly matching claim with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PFDNet is computationally more efficient than other methods that use multi-scale or multi-column architectures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PFDNet is computationally more efficient than other methods that use multi-scale or multi-column architectures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same content and certainty; minor label difference (conclusion vs contribution) does not change the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a perspective estimation branch allows PFDNet to be trained in either supervised or weakly-supervised settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a perspective estimation branch allows PFDNet to be trained in either supervised or weakly-supervised settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that PFDNet outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple datasets, achieving specific MAE values. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that PFDNet outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple datasets, achieving specific MAE values. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result and reported metrics are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel hybrid model that augments existing approaches using Long Short-Term Memory networks is suggested to predict system responses for fatigue test bench drive signals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two novel hybrid modeling strategies are suggested to improve system identification for fatigue test bench drive signals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim from a single novel hybrid model to two hybrid modeling strategies, broadening the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is tested using non-linear experimental data from a servo-hydraulic test rig. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Long Short-Term Memory networks improve the predictions of the hybrid models for non-linear responses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the observed improvement due to LSTMs on non-linear responses, which clarifies and specifies the original testing claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset used for testing the approach is made publicly available. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim states that the dataset was made publicly available; this appears omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A variety of metrics in time and frequency domains, as well as fatigue strength under variable amplitudes, are employed in the evaluation of the model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not enumerate the variety of evaluation metrics described in the preprint, so this specific claim is not present.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid models can successfully use frequency response function models as a linear baseline estimate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim about using frequency response function models as linear baseline is present in the publication but had no explicit counterpart in the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is applicable to measurement data with high sampling rates due to the windowing technique used. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a conclusion about applicability to high-sampling-rate measurement data that was not separately stated in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Twin neural network regression (TNNR) can be trained on unlabelled data points when labelled anchor data points are present. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Loops of predicted differences can be supplied to the training data even if the data points within loops are unlabelled. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the semi-supervised training capability in terms of loops of predicted differences, a more specific formulation of the preprint claim about training on unlabeled data with anchors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TNNR predicts differences between the target values of two different data points rather than the targets themselves. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Twin neural network regression (TNNR) predicts differences between the target values of two different data points. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same core claim about predicting differences is preserved with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ensembling predicted differences allows for very accurate predictions in the original regression problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensembling predicted differences allows for very accurate predictions for the original regression problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the ensembling result verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Semi-supervised training significantly improves the performance of TNNR, which is already state of the art. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Semi-supervised training significantly improves TNNR performance, which is already state of the art. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.
